title: Intensely Crunchy Canadian Chicken Tagine
description: A sour and crunchy Canadian dish featuring vin-santo and cooked in a cataplana vessel, this tagine is a quick-fix meal option that is also FODMAP-friendly.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, whisk together vin-santo, olive oil, lemon juice, and garlic-infused oil.
Add the chicken to the bowl and toss to coat with the marinade.
Transfer the chicken to the cataplana vessel and top with dried cranberries and chopped almonds.
Season with salt and black pepper.
Cover the cataplana and bake for 25 minutes.
Remove the cover and continue baking for an additional 10 minutes, or until the chicken is cooked through and the topping is crunchy.
Remove from the oven and let rest for 5 minutes before serving.
